title: Locating and Identifying UNIX Commands
description: A flowchart depicting the process a user (starting as root) follows to determine the type and location of a command using `type`, `which`, and `whereis`.
relationships: User enters a command (e.g., 'type ls', 'which cp', 'whereis grep')
Shell checks if command is an alias, builtin, or executable
type returns command's interpretation by the shell
whereis searches standard binary, man page, and source directories
Output reveals command type and location on the filesystem
